body:
## Problem

The 10 individual gear item pages (e.g. `/gear/tama-iron-cobra-900-double-pedal`, `/gear/zildjian-a-custom-series-cymbals`) serve the **generic fallback title/meta** from `api/meta/[...path].js`.

The current code at line 778 matches `/gear/([a-z-]+)` and looks up in `GEAR_CATEGORY_META` (which has only 6 entries: cymbals, snares, drums, pedals, sticks, hardware). Gear item slugs like `tama-iron-cobra-900-double-pedal` are NOT in that lookup → they fall through to the default SPA fallback:
- Title: `MetalForge | Discover What Metal Drummers Play`
- Description: none
- Schema: none (Product schema exists client-side but is invisible to crawlers)

Related but distinct from:
- #1300 (/gear hub SSR meta — approved, different page)
- #1301 (/gear/<category-slug> SSR meta — approved, different pages)

## Fix

**File:** `api/meta/[...path].js`

Add a lookup map for gear items sourced from the existing `gearItems` array (in `api/sitemap.js` — mirror the data or import from a shared location):

```js
const GEAR_ITEM_META = {
  'tama-iron-cobra-900-double-pedal': {
    name: 'Tama Iron Cobra 900 Double Pedal',
    category: 'double bass pedal',
    description: 'The Tama Iron Cobra 900 double pedal used by pro metal drummers like Eloy Casagrande and Tomas Haake.',
  },
  'meinl-byzance-series-cymbals': {
    name: 'Meinl Byzance Series Cymbals',
    category: 'cymbals',
    description: 'Meinl Byzance cymbals — favored by Tomas Haake, Matt Halpern, and top progressive metal drummers.',
  },
  // ... all 10 gear item slugs
};

const gearItemMatch = path.match(/^\/gear\/([a-z0-9-]+)$/);
if (gearItemMatch) {
  const slug = gearItemMatch[1];
  const item = GEAR_ITEM_META[slug];
  if (item) {
    return {
      title: `${item.name} — Used by Pro Metal Drummers | ${SITE_NAME}`,
      description: item.description,
      image: DEFAULT_IMAGE,
      type: 'website',
      url: `${BASE_URL}/gear/${slug}`,
      articleSchema: JSON.stringify({
        '@context': 'https://schema.org',
        '@type': 'BreadcrumbList',
        itemListElement: [
          { '@type': 'ListItem', position: 1, name: 'Home', item: BASE_URL },
          { '@type': 'ListItem', position: 2, name: 'Gear', item: `${BASE_URL}/gear` },
          { '@type': 'ListItem', position: 3, name: item.name, item: `${BASE_URL}/gear/${slug}` },
        ],
      }),
    };
  }
}
```

Place this handler **before** the existing `gearCategoryMatch` block (so item slugs are matched first, preventing fallthrough).

## 10 gear item slugs to cover
From `api/sitemap.js` gearItems array:
1. tama-iron-cobra-900-double-pedal
2. meinl-byzance-series-cymbals
3. paiste-rude-series-cymbals
4. zildjian-a-custom-series-cymbals
5. vic-firth-american-classic-5b
6. pearl-demon-drive-double-pedal
7. tama-starclassic-walnut-birch-drums
8. pearl-reference-series-drums
9. sonor-sq2-heavy-beech-drums
10. sabian-hhx-series-cymbals

## GSC query bucket served
- "[brand] [product] review"
- "[product name] metal drummers"
- "best [category] for metal drumming"
Purchase-intent queries that compound the gear hub (#1300/#1301).

## Quality gate
- Each of the 10 pages returns a keyword-matched title containing product name
- BreadcrumbList JSON-LD present in SSR response
- Product schema still present client-side (no regression)

## Verify
1. `curl -s https://metalforge.io/api/meta/gear/tama-iron-cobra-900-double-pedal | grep title` contains "Tama Iron Cobra"
2. `curl -s https://metalforge.io/api/meta/gear/zildjian-a-custom-series-cymbals | grep title` contains "Zildjian"
3. BreadcrumbList present in response JSON (grep 'BreadcrumbList')
4. All 10 gear item pages return 200
5. Google Rich Results Test on a gear item page shows BreadcrumbList

## Done
- [ ] `GEAR_ITEM_META` map added to `api/meta/[...path].js` with all 10 entries
- [ ] Handler added BEFORE gearCategoryMatch block
- [ ] Verified: SSR titles correct for all 10 gear items
title: SEO batch: Gear item pages (/gear/<item-slug>) missing SSR meta — ~10 pages serve generic fallback
